requirement.original: 加分太严重了，应该是最好的分数是+5，最差的分数是-40。agent是否知道自己的分数？此外用户打分现在每次只展示543，不展示更低的，这是严重问题。所以不要用ask user question模式，而是用happy原生的option模式，在close最后再问用户，而不是在运行close的时候。最后就是为了让用户有充分的参考，应该在close的时候自我总结这个session做了什么没做什么，满足了用户什么需求，没有满足什么，出现了哪些bug，出现了哪些改进空间。然后修复changelog-analyst bulk-commit bug (BULK=false时仍执行bulk行为) 和跨session commit干扰无隔离机制。
requirement.clarified: Six code changes: (1) rebalance EVENT_DELTAS so max positive=+5, min stays -40; (2) update close.md delta comments; (3) switch user rating from AskUserQuestion to <options> XML showing all 6 choices at end of /close output; (4) add mandatory session self-summary before rating; (5) fix changelog-analyst BULK=false staging to whitelist task files only; (6) add cross-session commit isolation guard.
requirement.what: Score rebalance, rating UX fix, session summary, bulk-commit bug fix, cross-session isolation
requirement.why: Positive scoring is too generous (+15 vs intended +5). AskUserQuestion hides 2-star and 1-star options. No session context for user rating. BULK=false stages all files like BULK=true. No cross-session file ownership isolation.

excluded: Negative delta changes (user says min stays -40)

success_criteria: Max positive EVENT_DELTA is +5; all negatives unchanged
All 6 rating choices visible to user via <options> XML
Session self-summary prints before rating prompt
Foreign-session files excluded from staging in normal mode
constraints: Positive deltas scaled proportionally (1/3 factor from 15 to 5)
<options> XML must appear at very end of /close text output

When dev-report exists but baseline_head_sha absent: whitelist and foreign-session exclusion still enforced, only provenance sanity check skipped. When no dev-report exists: stage-everything with WARNING.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Agent score awareness mechanism verified as working correctly (rank+range+recent events injected via prompt). No change needed.

Hook warns on <options> XML in Agent dispatch prompts during overnight mode. Not relevant here because <options> appears in /close's own text output (a command, not a dispatch prompt). No change needed.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Five distinct issues: inflated positive scoring, hidden rating options, missing session summary, BULK=false staging all files, cross-session commit interference.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Multiple independent design gaps: (1) EVENT_DELTAS max positive was +15 instead of user-intended +5; (2) AskUserQuestion tool has undocumented 4-option display limit; (3) no session summary was designed into /close; (4) changelog-analyst Phase 2 candidate set includes ALL git-status files regardless of BULK flag; (5) dev-report files_modified is used for enrichment only, not staging scope.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — these are design gaps, not regressions
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Original design did not account for user's scoring preferences, AskUserQuestion limitations, or the need for staging scope differentiation between bulk and normal modes.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Scoring inflation makes the ranking system meaningless. Hidden rating options prevent low ratings. Missing summary prevents informed rating. Unbounded staging in normal mode causes cross-session commit pollution.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Discovered during Cycle 2/3 close of spec-20260518-225715.
observations: EVENT_DELTAS max positive is +15 at score-update.sh:85 (inferred: Read)
AskUserQuestion is used for rating at close.md:380 with 6 choices listed, but user reports only 5/4/3 displayed (inferred: Read + user observation)
Phase 2 says 'Include all entries regardless of status code' at changelog-analyst.md:81-83 (inferred: Read)
dev.files_modified used 'for commit message enrichment only' at changelog-analyst.md:113-115 (inferred: Read)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Files read and lines cited above. User's complaint maps directly to measured code patterns.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression — these are design gaps in the original implementation

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Never use `git add -A` or `git add .` — always stage files individually
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents bulk staging via git add -A; individual file staging is required
Warns about files appearing after dispatch; warn-only, does NOT exclude

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Provenance filter (apply before using dev-report for enrichment)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Detects provenance anomalies but only excludes from enrichment, not staging

setup.reason: non-UI -- CLI script + agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: User reports: scoring too generous (max should be +5), rating shows only 5/4/3 (not 2/1), no session summary for rating context, BULK=false stages all files, cross-session commit interference.

measured.value: EVENT_DELTAS max positive = +15 (close_success_qa_pass.dev); AskUserQuestion used for rating with 6 choices; Phase 2 candidate set = all git-status files regardless of BULK flag; dev.files_modified used for enrichment only

expected.value: Max positive delta = +5; all 6 rating choices visible; BULK=false stages only task files; cross-session isolation enforced

evidence.gap: Positive deltas 3x too high; 2 of 6 rating options hidden; no session summary; no staging scope in normal mode; no cross-session isolation

reference_source.description: User verbatim requirement + source code Read verification

novelty_check.rationale: New requirements, no prior attempts to fix these specific issues

recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification

development_approach.strategy: Three parallel file edits: (1) score-update.sh EVENT_DELTAS rebalance; (2) close.md rating UX + session summary + delta comment updates; (3) changelog-analyst.md staging scope + isolation guard. Changes are independent and can be developed/verified separately.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies: (1) grep EVENT_DELTAS for max positive value; (2) grep close.md for AskUserQuestion absence and <options> presence; (3) read changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 for whitelist-based staging; (4) read close.md Step 3 for session summary section.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 7 issues with PROPOSED_FIX and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. 4 applied, 2 rejected (user requirement explicitly contradicts), 1 partially applied.

findings.issue: M3 conflicts with close-report final-line contract; <options> at very end would break CLOSE: parser
findings.proposed_fix: Specify two separate contracts: close-report retains CLOSE: final line; <options> goes in user-facing text message only
findings.rationale: Valid concern. Updated M3 and AC-03 to clarify <options> is in /close text message to user, NOT in close-report file.

findings.issue: Output-only <options> may lose synchronous score-update behavior; no post-option handling contract
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit post-option handling: machine-readable values, task-id retention, next selection triggers score-update calls
findings.rationale: Valid concern. Added post-option handling contract to M3 and AC-03.

findings.issue: M6 baseline-diff language can admit foreign-session files as inclusion source
findings.proposed_fix: Make dev.files_modified/created + task artifacts the primary whitelist; baseline diff only for provenance sanity check
findings.rationale: Valid concern. Updated M6 and AC-06 to make dev-report the primary whitelist, baseline diff only a sanity check.

findings.issue: Missing baseline_head_sha fallback reopens BULK=false bug when dev-report exists
findings.proposed_fix: If dev-report exists but baseline_head_sha absent, still apply dev-report whitelist; only skip provenance check
findings.rationale: Valid concern. Updated M5 and AC-05 to apply whitelist even when baseline_head_sha is absent.

findings.issue: +30 artifact buffer is brittle; can be too strict or too loose
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with computed deduped allowlist count
findings.rationale: User explicitly specified '+30' in decomposed requirement. The whitelist is the primary mechanism; +30 is a defense-in-depth abort guard, not a filter. Keeping as specified.

findings.issue: docs/dev files matching task-id pattern is ambiguous and can include sibling task files
findings.proposed_fix: Define anchored artifact matcher for known filename forms
findings.rationale: Valid concern. Updated M5 and AC-05 to use anchored artifact patterns instead of substring matching.

findings.issue: Bulk-mode isolation excluded despite broad cross-session requirement
findings.proposed_fix: Extend isolation to bulk mode or get user confirmation
findings.rationale: User's decomposed requirement explicitly says BULK=true behavior is unchanged. Bulk mode is intentionally an end-of-session sweep. Added explicit Won't Have entry.

findings.issue: M1 rounded values are internally consistent
findings.rationale: Values confirmed correct: 15->5, 8->3, 6->2, 3->1, 2->1, 1->0. user_rating_5 becomes neutral for BA/QA (ba=0, qa=0) which is consistent rounding.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 4 codex findings (final-line contract, post-option handling, baseline-diff scoping, anchored artifact patterns). Rejected 2 (user explicitly specified +30 buffer and BULK=true unchanged). Logged 1 observation.
